comparative_news.title: How to Compare Flex, Jabil, Sanmina, Celestica Manufacturing Supply Chain Capabilities
comparative_news.description: You can see how the combined savings from managing IDL accuracy, timeliness, and productivity in companies like Flex and Jabil…for the above roles can be considerable. Multiply these savings as a result of reducing IDL errors and driving IDL efficiencies for larger contract electronics providers with multiple factories around the world and employing tens of, even hundreds of, thousands in their IDL workforce and you quickly see how Sanmina’s claim of $50,000 in savings is myopic.

comparative_news.title: How to Compare Flex, Jabil, Sanmina, Celestica Manufacturing Supply Chain Capabilities | by Mark Zetter | Medium
comparative_news.description: Savings opportunities attributed to lower IDL costs for any manufacturer, any EMS provider, will far eclipse savings focused only on DL. Putting this in perspective, the average cost per hour for manufacturing DL is estimated at $23 (US). Contract electronics solutions companies like Flex, Jabil, Sanmina, and Celestica are paying IDL process engineers and manufacturing engineers US $60k to $80k, each year.

comparative_news.title: Celestica vs. Jabil: Which EMS Stock is a Better Bet Right Now?
comparative_news.description: However, Jabil operates in a highly competitive environment, facing competition from both domestic and international electronic manufacturers, manufacturing service providers and designers like Sanmina. The tense geopolitical situation between the United States and China and the wars in Europe and the Middle East remain headwinds. Against the backdrop of this global uncertainty, low demand in some consumer-centric markets is negatively impacting its margins. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for Celestica’s 2025 sales and EPS implies year-over-year growth of 11.7% and 23.2%, respectively.

comparative_news.title: EMS manufacturing industry status on automation and artificial intelligence | VentureOutsource.com
comparative_news.description: Flex is better positioned than both Jabil and Sanmina, and smarter if only because it spun off Elementum. But Flex also has made no real inroads into AI that I can find in speaking with customers, and former employees, of the Elementum platform.

comparative_news.description: Flex experienced double-digit revenue losses in 2024. Sanmina, with approximately $8 billion in revenue, specializes in high-quality, complex manufacturing for industries including medical, automotive, and telecommunications.

comparative_news.title: Celestica vs. Flex: Which EMS Stock Has Better Growth Potential? | Nasdaq
comparative_news.description: However, Flex operates in a highly competitive environment, facing competition from both domestic and international electronic manufacturers, manufacturing service providers and designers like Jabil and Sanmina. The tense geopolitical situation between the United States and China and the wars in Europe and the Middle East remain headwinds. Against the backdrop of this global uncertainty, low demand in some consumer-centric markets is negatively impacting its margins. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for Celestica’s 2025 sales and EPS implies year-over-year growth of 13.1% and 30.1%, respectively.

comparative_news.title: SANM vs. JBL: Which Electronics Manufacturing Stock is the Better Buy?
comparative_news.description: The company faces competition from Celestica and Flex LTD. FLEX in the EMS market. Flex is also rapidly expanding into the AI data center space. It also boasts a strong presence worldwide with a resilient supply chain. However, by expanding its AI native portfolio offering and strengthening its supply chain network, Jabil is well-positioned to gain a competitive edge. Strong focus on innovation, combined with product diversification, is a major aspect of Sanmina’s growth strategy.

comparative_news.title: Next generation manufacturing: PCB assembly companies face adaptive, technical challenges | VentureOutsource.com
comparative_news.description: These providers are considered tier-1s in EMS industry yet none of them have a full technology stack solution to offer OEM customers who hand them responsibility for OEM supply chain management. My earlier comparison of supply chain capabilities for Jabil, Sanmina, Flex, and Celestica goes into more detail and can be read here.
